name: Tasmania (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the disc bearing the state badge, also in the Union Jack
the lion passant of the state badge, also the crosses in the Union Jack

description: A blue British ensign with the Union Jack in the canton and a white disc in the fly bearing a red lion passant.
history: Adopted on 25 September 1876 and retained after Australian federation in 1901. The red lion is widely interpreted as a reference to the British coat of arms.

features.symbolism: Based on the British Blue Ensign, the standard format for Australian state flags.
the Union Jack, signifying historic ties to the United Kingdom

features.role: white disc bearing the red lion
features.symbolism: the white disc serves as the field for the state badge

features.role: red lion passant within the white disc
features.symbolism: the red lion, recalling British heritage and royal authority
